raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The free market is driven by profit motive, and sky-high housing prices are forcing Californians to move away from their home communities.",
    "Public sector intervention is necessary to help tackle the housing crisis.",
    "AB 2881 will create the California Housing Authority with the mission of producing social housing developments for residents.",
    "Social housing is financially self-sustaining because people with higher incomes will subsidize low-income units.",
    "Social housing avoids the problem of concentrated poverty by creating mixed-income neighborhoods.",
    "Social housing fosters economic opportunities while preserving affordability in the community.",
    "Residents of social housing will be granted protections similar to those in private properties, including protection against arbitrary termination.",
    "Social housing has been successful both nationally and internationally.",
    "About 80% of residents in Singapore live in social housing flats, and 9 in 10 of these residents own their flats.",
    "62% of residents in Vienna live in social housing, and 75% of residents qualify based on income.",
    "The population of people experiencing homelessness in California grew by 30.5% from 2007 to 2023.",
    "California needs to build at least 2.5 million more housing units by 2030, including 1 million homes affordable to lower-income households.",
    "California built roughly 85,000 housing units annually from 2018 to 2022, including about 15,000 lower-income units.",
    "The mixed-income model of social housing is gaining momentum in the U.S.",
    "Montgomery County, Maryland, and the Capitol Area Development Authority in Sacramento have successfully implemented the mixed-income model.",
    "Seattle passed a ballot initiative to create a social housing developer last year.",
    "Legislation has been recently introduced in New York State to establish a social housing development authority."
  ]
}
```
